title: Chatter Hiring SOP
subtitle: Recruitment and Vetting Manual

blocks.content: Chatters are the human layer of the system. They control communication, emotions, and revenue. A bad hire does not just reduce income. A bad hire can damage accounts, leak methods, or sabotage operations.
Hiring is not about finding cheap labor. It is about finding reliable operators who can follow structure and respect boundaries. You are not hiring personalities. You are hiring discipline.

blocks.title: Core Rule
blocks.content: Every chatter must be filtered. Every chatter must be tested. Every chatter must be proven before trust is given.

left.content: These platforms provide higher quality applicants who already understand online work and adult industry communication.

items: Telegram groups such as ofmjobs on Telegram

right.content: Telegram is usable but lower quality. Many applicants there lack professionalism, discipline, or long term commitment.

right.title: Rule
right.content: The more public and unstructured the platform, the lower the average candidate quality. Always prioritize platforms where people apply formally.

blocks.content: Before any interview, applicants must be screened. This stage is about eliminating obvious bad fits before wasting time on interviews.

items: Basic English level from their written messages

blocks.title: Filter Rule
blocks.content: If a candidate cannot write clearly, politely, and in full sentences, they should not move forward.
Every candidate must go through a live interview. The interview is not just about answers. It is about behavior.

items: Experience with adult platforms if any
How confident they are in English
How honest they seem about past work

right.title: Rejection Rule
right.content: Candidates who avoid direct answers or exaggerate experience should be rejected.

blocks.content: After the interview, every candidate must be tested live. They must join a Discord call with screen sharing.

items: Must write sample replies in chat
Prevents cheating with translators or AI tools
Words per minute test done live
Shows how fast they can respond to fans

right.title: Rejection Rule
right.content: If a candidate refuses screen sharing or avoids live testing, they should be rejected immediately.

blocks.content: No candidate should get direct access after testing. They must first observe trained chatters for one to two days.
This stage filters out people who lose interest quickly or cannot focus. Only candidates who stay attentive and engaged should move forward.
Access must be earned gradually. New chatters should never receive full account access immediately. This reduces damage if a hire turns out to be malicious or incompetent.

items: First stage: Read only access, observation only
Second stage: Limited supervised chatting, no financial controls
Final stage: Full chatting access after trust is built
Clear payment rules must be set before work begins
Never change pay terms without agreement

right.title: Trust Rule
right.content:
A chatter who is paid on time is more loyal.
A chatter who is not paid becomes a risk.

left.content: Self reporting saves management time but requires verification to prevent manipulation.

right.content: You must regularly verify these stats to ensure they are accurate.

blocks.content: Chatters can cause serious damage if mistreated. They can leak methods, sabotage accounts, and spread negative reputation. Fear based management creates short term compliance but long term risk.

right.title: Trust Principle
right.content:
Trust is built through fairness, consistency, and transparency.
A well treated chatter protects your system.
A mistreated chatter becomes a threat.

summary_points: Chatters are operators — their performance directly affects profit and risk
Hiring correctly increases stability and revenue
Hiring poorly increases loss and exposure
Live interviews, live English testing, and controlled access are not optional
Most losses come from rushing hires, skipping tests, and trusting without verification
Payment must be on time — delayed pay creates risk
Structure separates scalable operations from chaos
Treat chatters with respect — they control your revenue
